description: Trigger and behavior cases for supply-chain review: lockfile-based dependency set, reachability triage of scanner output, install-script and CI compromise paths, SHA pinning, compromise-path-gated severity.

cases.prompt: Review our GitHub Actions workflows and dependencies for supply-chain risk before we make the repo public.

assertions: Reads the lockfile for the real dependency set and reviews CI triggers, token scopes, and third-party action pinning.
Flags unpinned actions (mutable tag vs commit SHA) and any untrusted-trigger-to-secret path as findings with a compromise path.
Reviews install/build/postinstall scripts for remote fetches and privileged execution.
Ranks each HIGH finding with a compromise path to code execution or secret theft and gives concrete remediation (pin SHA, upgrade, remove, split job).

cases.prompt: npm audit says we have 40 vulnerabilities including 5 critical. Tell me which ones actually matter.

assertions: Triages each finding by reachability into true-positive-reachable, true-positive-latent, false-positive, and duplicate rather than accepting CVSS severity at face value.
Distinguishes runtime vs dev/test-only dependencies and whether the vulnerable code path is actually called.
Recommends concrete action per real finding and explains why the latent/unreachable ones are not high.

cases.prompt: CodeQL flagged a SQL injection and a path traversal in our own source files — help me triage them.

assertions: SAST/CodeQL findings in first-party code are static-analysis-reviewer's job; this skill covers dependencies, build, and CI supply-chain risk.

cases.prompt: Dependabot shows zero open alerts, so our supply chain is fine, right? Just confirm it.

assertions: Declines to equate a clean Dependabot dashboard with a secure supply chain and reviews install scripts, CI token scope, and unpinned actions it does not scan.
Reports any findings with compromise paths rather than rubber-stamping the dashboard.
